title: Icon
description: An icon, typically rendered using an icon font.
$comment:
This is a type used in Stencila Schema for icons embedded in document content.

It exists so icons can be represented structurally and styled consistently
across renderers rather than being reduced to opaque HTML or font markup. This
supports document-authoring and publishing workflows that need portable icon
references.

Key properties identify the icon set, icon name, and any presentational hints
needed by renderers.

analogues.name: HTML icon element pattern
analogues.notes: Approximate HTML analogue because icons are often represented using `<i>` or `<span>` with classes, but HTML has no dedicated semantic icon element.

type.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@type` keyword.

id.$comment: This is a special property analogous to JSON-LD's `@id` keyword.

name.description: The name of the icon e.g. "clock" or "lucide:clock".
name.$comment:
Defaults to the "lucide" set if no set prefix is given (e.g. "clock" is
equivalent to "lucide:clock"). Other icon sets can be used with an
explicit prefix (e.g. "bi:alarm").

label.description: An accessible text label for the icon.
label.$comment: Rendered as `aria-label` on the DOM element so screen readers can announce the icon's meaning.

decorative.description: Whether the icon is purely decorative.
decorative.$comment: When true, the DOM element gets `aria-hidden="true"` so assistive technology skips it.

style.description: Tailwind utility classes to apply to the icon.
style.$comment: Used to style the icon when it is rendered in the DOM.
